text.title: Draft Mode + Bot Identity + CODEOWNERS Gate
text.summary: Auto-opened PRs from an error tracker MUST be drafts authored by a dedicated bot identity (GitHub App or `*-bot` user), labelled `agent:<tracker>`, and merge-blocked until at least one CODEOWNERS-listed human approves.

section.title: Three-layer merge gate
section.p: Each layer is necessary on its own: draft state stops accidental clicks during triage; the dedicated bot identity makes audit logs filterable and lets branch-protection target bot-authored commits specifically; the CODEOWNERS rule forces human accountability on every patch. None of the three on its own is enough — bots have shipped self-approved PRs whenever any layer is missing.

rule.statement: The agent MUST open the PR with `draft: true`. The author MUST be a dedicated identity (GitHub App preferred over PAT, or `seer-by-sentry[bot]` / `bugsink-bot` / equivalent) — never the human who installed the integration. The PR MUST carry the label `agent:<tracker>` (e.g., `agent:seer`, `agent:rollbar`). Branch protection MUST require at least one approving review from a CODEOWNERS-listed human on agent-authored commits, with `Require approval of the most recent reviewable push` enabled so any new commit by the agent re-triggers review. A merge by the bot identity MUST be denied by repository policy.
rule.rationale: GitHub does not provide first-class "bot-only path" branch protection; the combination of bypass-list (NOT containing the bot), `Require approval of most recent push`, and a label-aware policy bot (palantir/policy-bot or label-based-pr-policy-action) is the documented workaround. Microsoft's Azure governance pattern and igotasite4that's coding-agent security write-up converge on this exact triad.

example.code: # .github/policy-bot.yml policy: approval: - or: - human review on agent PR rules: - name: human review on agent PR if: has_author_in: users: ["seer-by-sentry[bot]", "bugsink-bot", "rollbar-app[bot]"] has_labels: ["agent:seer", "agent:bugsink", "agent:rollbar"] requires: count: 1 teams: ["acme/maintainers"]
example.note: Label + bot author + CODEOWNERS team form the deny-by-default gate.

example.code: # branch-protection: only required_approving_review_count: 1 # (no bot detection, no label, no "most recent push" rule) required_approving_review_count: 1
example.note: Bot can be on the bypass list, or the agent's own +1 from a teammate counts.

antipattern.description: Agent opens the PR as ready-for-review with `auto_merge: true` and "we trust CI to catch problems".
antipattern.reason: CI confirms tests pass; it cannot judge whether the patch addresses the right alert or introduces a security regression. The draft state plus human review is the only place that judgement lives.

section.reference: Org config showing `pr_mode: draft` and required label.
GitHub community discussion: Copilot agent + n+1 approvals.
Microsoft Cloud Adoption Framework — AI agent governance.
Securing AI coding agent workflows (sandbox + review gates).
